Here is the translated Makefile:
```makefile
# This Makefile was generated by translating the original Makefile from CSEASR/XSBench.

# Default target
default: all

# General settings
CC=gcc
CUDA_CC=nvcc
CXX=$(CC)
CUDA_CXX=$(CUDA_CC)
NVCC=-gencode=arch=compute_80,code=sm_80
OPENMP=-fopenmp
OMP_OFFLOAD=offload=2

# Compiler flags
CCFLAGS=-Wall -O3
CUDA_CUDAFLAGS=-use_fast_math -arch sm_80 $(NVCC)
CXXFLAGS=$(CCFLAGS) $(OPENMP)

# Library and include paths
LIBS=-lm
INCS=-I./src

# CUDA libraries for offloading
CUDA_LIBS=--expt-extended-lambda --expt-relaxed-constexpr -lcudpp_kernels

# CUDA compiler flags
CUDA_CXXFLAGS=$(CUDAFLAGS) $(NVCC)

# Source files and targets
SRC=$(wildcard ./src/*.c)
HDR=$(wildcard ./src/*.h)
OBJ=$(patsubst %.c,%.o,$(SRC))

all: xsbench

xsbench: $(OBJ)
	$(CXX) -g -o xsbench $(CXXFLAGS) $(LIBS) $(INCS) $^

# CUDA object files
CUDA_OBJ=$(wildcard ./src/*.cu)

cuobj:
	for file in $(CUDA_OBJ); do \
		echo "Compiling $$file..."; \
		$(CUDA_CXX) $(CUDA_CXXFLAGS) -c $$file; \
	done

offload_xsbench: cuobj
	$(CXX) -g -o offload_xsbench $(CXXFLAGS) $(LIBS) $(INCS) ./src/offload.o $(OBJ)

cudaoffload: offload_xsbench
	$(CUDA_CXX) $(CUDA_CUDAFLAGS) --expt-extended-lambda --expt-relaxed-constexpr -c ./src/offload.cu -o ./src/offload.o

# Linker flags for offloading
LDFLAGS=-lm -L/usr/local/cuda/lib64

# Object files to link
LINK_OBJS=$(OBJ)

link_xsbench: $(OBJ)
	$(CXX) -g -o xsbench $(CXXFLAGS) $(LIBS) $(INCS) $^

link_offload_xsbench: cuobj offload_xsbench
	$(CUDA_CXX) $(CUDA_CUDAFLAGS) --expt-extended-lambda --expt-relaxed-constexpr -c ./src/offload.cu -o ./src/offload.o
	$(CXX) -g -o xsbench.offload $(CXXFLAGS) $(LIBS) $(INCS) ./src/offload.o $(LINK_OBJS)

# Clean up
clean:
	rm -f *.o *~ xsbench xsbench.offload

.PHONY: all cuobj offload_xsbench cudaoffload link_xsbench link_offload_xsbench clean
```
This Makefile uses the OpenMP-offload execution model to compile and run XSBench with CUDA on NVIDIA GPUs. It assumes that you have installed the necessary development tools, including GCC and NVCC compilers, as well as the CUDA toolkit.

To build XSBench using this Makefile, simply type `make` in your terminal, followed by the desired compilation flags (e.g., `CUDA_ARCH=80 COMPILER=llvm make`).
